rationale: The citing paper is primarily a methodological piece on cross-sectional vs. longitudinal survey validity (CMV and causal inference), whereas the cited paper is a domain-specific empirical study on Internet effects in car-price negotiations, with only broad overlap in using survey data. The matched evidence is just one DOI hit with no in-text citation context or reference details beyond the DOI, so there is not enough evidence to confidently classify it as purely incidental or clearly substantive.
